By working on the Lithium Data Science team at Albemarle Corporation , I'm helping enable electrification and the transition to EVs-- making a positive impact on the world. #DataScience is all about asking questions and thinking critically, and I'm challenged everyday to consider the current and future implications of topics related to the EV supply chain. 

The LAUNCH program has allowed me to gain experiences beyond my degree and has pushed me outside my comfort zone. Diving into a new role (Data Science) with new software and tools ( #Python , #GitHub , etc.) in a new industry (Chemical Manufacturing) has been a major learning curve, but these are exactly the types of situations that jumpstart growth. 

I am extremely grateful to Albemarle, the LAUNCH Program, and numerous colleagues + managers who have supported me and helped me achieve these goals. 

So here's a plug to write down your goals, reflect back on them, and be encouraged about how much you've grown. Looks like it's time for me to consider what I want to accomplish in the next 2 years!
...

This ALB stock analysis is built on the 25+ alternative data signals AltIndex tracks on Albemarle Corporation every day — hiring, web traffic, sentiment, social reach — rolled into an AI Score of 38/100: a Sell. The stock trades at $135.56 with a $16B market cap.
...

Our Product Safety Data Analytics team is seeking an experienced AI/ML Data Scientist with hands-on experience in the full end to end data science lifecycle for artificial intelligence & machine learning.

refine storytelling skills to

results.title: LLM Engineer job with VIB | 12860950
highlights:
LLM Engineer job with VIB | 12860950
...
# LLM Engineer
...
VIB is a leading life sciences research institute in Flanders, Belgium, with a strong focus on translating basic scientific discoveries into benefits for society. With the launch of VIB.AI, we are expanding our mission to harness the power of artificial intelligence for life sciences research, innovation, and impact.
...
The VIB Center for Neuroscience Leuven (CNL) investigates the mechanisms of brain function and neurological disease across the scales of biology, spanning neurodegeneration, neurodevelopment, synaptic biology, and computational neuroscience. AI and large language models have transformative potential across these research areas.
...
We are now looking for an LLM Engineer in a shared position between VIB.AI's Machine Learning Expertise Unit and the Center for Neuroscience. You will split your time between building institute-wide AI tools and applying them directly to neuroscience research challenges.
...
As an LLM Engineer in this shared position, you will:
...
- Develop and deploy LLM-based pilot projects tailored to neuroscience research at the CNL, applying AI to questions in molecular, cellular and circuit neuroscience, neurodegeneration, neurodevelopment and disease mechanisms.
...
- Build and maintain components of VIB's institutional intelligence layer, including agentic bioinformatics workflows, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems, and AI-assisted experimental design tools.
...
- Design, implement, and evaluate LLM-based solutions for scientific text mining, multimodal integration of literature and biological data, and knowledge extraction to support discovery.
...
- Collaborate with wet-lab and computational neuroscience researchers to identify opportunities where generative AI and LLMs can accelerate scientific progress.
...
- Work with the Machine Learning Expertise Unit and VIB's AI Studio to translate pilot projects into practical tools that serve the broader VIB community.
...
- Promote ethical, transparent, and responsible use of LLMs in line with best practices and international standards.
...
- Mine opportunities and data at the center’s advanced technology expertise centers and affiliated central core facilities.
...
- Leverage VIB's Data Core services, including high-performance computing pipelines and large-scale GPU resources, to scale LLM development and deployment.
...
- PhD in machine learning, computer science, computational biology, computational neuroscience, statistics, or a related field.
- Strong experience with large language models, including pretraining, fine-tuning, prompt engineering, and evaluation.
- Knowledge of modern deep learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, JAX, TensorFlow).
- Experience with real-world applications of LLMs in scientific or biomedical contexts is a plus.
...
- Familiarity with neuroscience data types (imaging, electrophysiology, genomics, proteomics, or clinical data) is a plus but not required.
...
- Ability to work collaboratively across disciplines and communicate effectively with researchers from different backgrounds.
...
- A strong interest in applying LLMs to impactful life science and neuroscience problems.
...
and high-performance
...
scale GPU resources.
...
For further information, please send an email to stein.aerts@kuleuven.be or joost.schymkowitz@kuleuven.be with subject title Inquiry: LLM Engineer CNL/VIB.AI.

results.title: Principal Applied AI Engineer (Hybrid) job with AbbVie | 3058409
highlights:
Principal Applied AI Engineer (Hybrid) job with AbbVie | 3058409
...
# Principal Applied AI Engineer (Hybrid)
...
Employer AbbVie Location North Chicago, IL Salary 124500 - 236500 USD Start date Jul 1, 2026
...
Discipline Engineering, Information Technology, Applications/Technical Support, Science/R&D, Machine Learning Required Education Bachelors Degree Position Type Full time Hotbed BioMidwest, Best Places to Work
...
## Job Details
...
The Applied AI Engineer within the Operations Transformation group will be responsible for designing, developing, and implementing AI and machine learning solutions to drive innovation and operational excellence across AbbVies manufacturing, supply chain, and quality operations. This role will serve as a translator between technical programming teams and functional business leaders and will work closely with cross-functional teams including Operations, Engineering, IT, and Data Science to deliver AI-powered improvements in efficiency, quality, and agility in a regulated environment.
...
Key Responsibilities
...
- Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders to identify, prioritize, and define opportunities to apply AI and machine learning to operations challenges (e.g., predictive maintenance, process optimization, quality monitoring)
...
- Map, analyze, and assess current-state business processes to identify inefficiencies and improvement opportunities, and to inform where AI-enabled solutions can drive the most value
...
- Translate operations needs into clear AI/ML problem statements and success metrics
...
- Partner with data science, engineering, and IT teams to select appropriate algorithms, technologies, and tools and ensure solutions meet business and compliance needs
...
- Support vendor assessment, selection and drive prioritized use cases from initial scoping through technical proof of concept in partnership with engineering, IT, and external vendors
...
- Partner with automation, robotics, and digital teams to enable intelligent process automation and advanced analytics
...
- Integrate AI/ML solutions with operations data platforms (MES, SCADA, ERP, IoT) and ensure seamless deployment in production environments
...
- Establish robust documentation, testing, monitoring, and model management practices to ensure AI solutions remain reliable, explainable, and compliant (GxP, FDA, data privacy)
...
- Participate in AI governance, best practice development, and continuous improvement initiatives
...
- Support change management, training, and knowledge transfer to enable successful adoption of AI capabilities within operations
...
- Stay up-to-date with emerging trends in AI, ML, and manufacturing digital transformations relevant to pharmaceutical operations
...
- Bachelors or Masters degree in Business, Operations, Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, or another relevant field, or equivalent practical experience. Advanced degree preferred.
...
- 5+ years of experience building and deploying AI/ML solutions, preferably in manufacturing, supply chain, or quality environments.
...
- Strong understanding of AI and machine learning concepts, with prior experience applying them in real-world settings to solve operational or business problems; able to partner effectively with hands-on engineering teams for implementation and deployment.
...
- Experience integrating AI/ML models into operational/IT systems (MES, SCADA, IoT, ERP, cloud).
...
- Strong problem-solving, project management, and communication skills.
...
- Familiarity with GxP, FDA, and data privacy requirements is a plus.

highlights:
Principal Data Scientist, Gen AI Application | Joblume

# Principal Data Scientist, Gen AI Application
...
Remote, USA
...
Posted Jun 22, 2026
...
Full-time
...
About the position
...
The PositionWhy GenentechWe're passionate about delivering on Our Promise to improve the lives of patients and create healthier communities for all. We foster a culture of inclusivity, integrity and creativity while boldly pursuing answers to the world's most complex health challenges and transforming society.Genentech's Data, Digital, and Analytics (DDA) team is dedicated to solving complex healthcare challenges and improving patient outcomes. DDA empowers business partners across Commercial, Medical, and Government Affairs (CMG) to make impactful decisions by leveraging data, analytics, and AI/ML to enable fast, targeted actions in rapidly evolving business contexts.DDA fosters a unified understanding of customers, actions, and outcomes by transforming the business insight supply chain from the traditional reactive service model to a modern proactive product model, which integrates analytics and insights seamlessly into CMG's evolving digital, data, and automation platforms, creating scalable solutions and eliminating silos. In DDA, you will work as a trusted, objective advisor and expert, recommending critical decisions and actions to be taken with credibility and a focus on driving measurable impact. You will be part of a diverse, inclusive team that reflects the world we serve, thriving in a welcoming culture built on collaboration and innovation.The OpportunityThe Sr. Data Scientist - Generative AI Application Specialist is responsible for building fundamental agentic capabilities and high-level business applications within a fast-moving, experimental environment. This role bridges the gap between technical infrastructure and user experience, collaborating with infrastructure teams to design agents and partnering with product owners to ensure optimal development approaches for desired UX. As a subject matter expert, you will stay at the forefront of Generative AI models and capabilities, influencing technical priorities in a complex enterprise environment while representing the team across the global Roche organization.

# Data Scientist (100%)

results.title: Distinguished Data Scientist: Associate AI Experience at Walmart | Jeremy
highlights:
# Distinguished Data Scientist: Associate AI Experience
...
Distinguished individual contributor role leading next-generation agentic AI systems and autonomous agents at enterprise scale across Walmart's global operations, combining deep ML/AI expertise with cross-functional mentorship and high-impact productionized solutions.
...
We are seeking a Distinguished Data Scientist to provide technical leadership and long‑term vision for next‑generation AI systems and platforms. This senior individual contributor will drive breakthrough system design and advanced solution development that transforms Walmart at scale. The role combines deep technical expertise (ML, simulation, optimization, agentic AI) with cross‑functional influence, mentoring of senior talent, and delivery of high‑impact, productionized solutions. You will be a great fit if: - You architect end-to-end ML/AI systems — from feature engineering through production deployment and monitoring - You’ve built and shipped multiple autonomous AI agents (not just experimented with LLM APIs) - You’re Level 5+ on Steve Yegge’s Vibe Coding scale and can ship production systems through AI-assisted development - You combine deep statistical rigor with practical business impact
...
About the Team The Associate AI Experiences team applies advanced data science, machine learning, and agentic AI to solve high-impact business problems across Walmart globally. You’ll build production Agentic systems, deploy autonomous AI agents, and translate complex data into actionable insights that drive measurable business value at massive scale. Our team builds intelligent platforms, agents and ML-powered solutions that make associates’ jobs easier, faster, and more fulfilling across stores, fulfillment centers, distribution centers, and home office. We leverage the Element LLM Gateway to safely access state-of-the-art language models while keeping all data within Walmart’s network. Our work spans NLP, computer vision, time series forecasting, and multi-agent AI systems at enterprise scale.

highlights:

...
# Pfizer is hiring a Principal ML/AI Cheminformatics Scientist
...
As a Principal ML/AI Cheminformatics Data Scientist within the Computational Absorption, Distribution, Metabolism, and Excretion (cADME) group in Pfizer’s Pharmacokinetics, Dynamics, and Metabolism (PDM) organization, you will apply data-driven approaches to address critical challenges in small-molecule drug discovery.
...
In this role, you will develop, deploy, and continuously improve machine learning models for molecular property prediction and leverage Pfizer’s proprietary chemical and biological datasets to inform decision-making across hit-to-lead and lead optimization stages. Your work will directly enable more efficient compound prioritization, improved prediction of ADME properties, and accelerated progression of discovery programs.
...
Success in this position will be defined by the delivery of robust, validated computational models and workflows that generate actionable insights and measurable impact on project outcomes. You will collaborate closely with cross-functional PDM partners and external collaborators to integrate AI-driven approaches into discovery workflows and maximize the value of Pfizer’s industry-leading data assets.
...
Drive scientific and technical advancement in PDM’s AI and cheminformatics capabilities by developing and delivering robust, validated workflows and algorithms that directly support drug discovery. Success will be measured by the implementation of effective AI-driven solutions, demonstrated impact on project outcomes, and the publication or presentation of results.
...
Design, build, and deploy innovative cheminformatics and AI workflows for small molecule discovery, with clear deliverables including prioritized compound lists, well-defined chemical libraries, and actionable structure-activity relationship analyses.
...
Collaborate closely with medicinal chemists, biologists, and data scientists to integrate computational approaches into experimental and analytical workflows. Success in this
...
will be reflected by joint project milestones achieved, co-authored publications, and the creation of interdisciplinary solutions that accelerate drug discovery.

Continuously monitor and evaluate emerging advances in AI, cheminformatics, and computational drug discovery, proactively leading the adoption of promising new methodologies. Contributions will include piloting novel techniques, leading internal workshops or training sessions, and helping set strategic directions for technology adoption.

data scientists—

computational solutions into drug discovery workflows.

Experience applying AI methods to medicinal chemistry or cheminformatics challenges, including work with molecular representations, chemical fingerprints, generative models, transformer architectures, pretraining strategies, property prediction, virtual screening, and ADMET modeling.

Machine Learning (ML) Engineer at VIB.AI job with VIB | 12860981
...
# Machine Learning (ML) Engineer at VIB.AI
...
Application deadline: August 31, 2026 About us VIB is a leading life sciences research institute in Flanders, Belgium, with a strong focus on translating basic scientific discoveries into benefits for society. With the launch of VIB.AI, we are expanding our mission to harness the power of artificial intelligence for life sciences research, innovation, and impact. We are now looking for a Machine Learning (ML) Engineer to join the Machine Learning Expertise Unit at VIB.AI. Your role As an ML Engineer, you will: Develop and deploy ML solutions in close collaboration with researchers across VIB centers, applying state-of-the-art methods to cutting-edge biomedical questions.Design, implement, and evaluate ML and foundation-model approaches, including LLM-based solutions, for scientific text mining, multimodal integration of literature and biological data, and knowledge extraction to support discovery.Collaborate with researchers to identify opportunities where generative AI can accelerate scientific progress.Work within the Machine Learning Expertise Unit and with VIB AI Studio to translate pilot projects into practical tools.Promote ethical, transparent, and responsible use of AI in line with best practices and international standards.Leverage VIB Data Core services, including high-performance computing pipelines and large-scale GPU resources, to scale ML development and deployment. Your profile PhD in machine learning, computer science, computational biology, statistics, or a related field.Knowledge of modern deep learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, JAX, TensorFlow).Experience developing AI applications for scientific or biomedical research, including LLM-based and agentic AI systems, is a plus.Ability to work collaboratively across disciplines and communicate effectively with researchers from different backgrounds.A strong interest in applying ML to impactful life science problems.We welcome applications from both early-career and experienced ML engineers. Our offer A stimulating, international, interdisciplinary, collaborative, and supportive work environment, which emphasizes diversity and inclusion.An environment that promotes personal development.The opportunity to pioneer applications of ML in biomedical sciences.Direct access to VIB’s Data Core services, which provide expert support in data management and high-performance computing, including optimized pipelines and large-scale GPU resources.A competitive salary and benefits package, with relocation support if needed.The chance to make a lasting impact on science, health, and society. About VIB.AI VIB.AI is the artificial intelligence initiative of VIB, designed to accelerate breakthroughs in life sciences by integrating advanced AI methods into research, discovery, and innovation. For more information, visit vib.ai/en. How to apply Please complete the application through the VIB online application tool (https://jobs.vib.be/apply/135914). A complete application file should contain the following documents: Your motivation letter stating career goals, past experience, and how these relate to the current position.Your CV.Contact information of three references. Application deadline: August 31st 2026. For further information, please send an email to pedro.goncalves@kuleuven.be, dmitry.kobak@ugent.be or stein.aerts@kuleuven.be with subject title Inquiry: ML Engineer.
